title: Can anyone tell me if they have used D.O.T trading and if it is legit
author: Calm-Associate-3274
selftext: Just want to know. No sarcastic or nasty comments please I just want straight answers. Thanks

comments.author: Rude_Quiet_6676
comments.body:
I’ve just started trading 
Will let you know in a week

replies.author: Calm-Associate-3274
replies.body: Thanks for the response. It looks promising. Returns aren't staggering but that makes me feel it could be more trustworthy. Fingers crossed for you

replies.author: Calm-Associate-3274
replies.body: Any news?

replies.author: digots
replies.body: How was your first week?

comments.author: digots
comments.body:
I started this week.  Not encouraging, 3 losses, not massive, total down about 10%, started at a bad time.  But they seem to double down on losses, doubling the trade size with no SL set, seems a bit risky.

Will monitor and report back.

replies.author: Calm-Associate-3274
replies.body: Hiya thanks for your response. I also started Tuesday and experienced 3 losses and 1 win. The problem is it was one trade a day but they doubled up each day like you said and when asked they said because they were trying to make a recovery?? I was disappointed because the reviews were all so positive. Today 1 loss and 1 win with the loss outweighing the win. I don't know whether to pull my money out now or keep going. Something is telling me to pull as I have a feeling it's just going to get worse...

replies.author: digots
replies.body:
Yeah, loss recovery is not a good strategy at all, martingale until you are bust, no SL.  Gonna leave a negative on trustpilot as they are taking the piss.  Will take out most of my money and up the multiplier on copytrade on smaller pot, then withdraw if they have a streak.

Joining another group who are able to chat with each other, trader teachers how to trade and why, much safer.

replies.author: Calm-Associate-3274
replies.body: Can you let me know this group please. I will do the same. Thank you also for your honesty

replies.author: digots
replies.body: PM'd you.

replies.author: billybob2917
replies.body: Could I also have the name of this group? I was on verge of joining DOT until I saw this. Thanks.

replies.author: digots
replies.body: sent

replies.author: babylawyer86
replies.body: Could I also please get the name of this group - thanks

replies.author: mowoopas1980
replies.body: Could I get that name as well if possible

replies.author: digots
replies.body: [https://coleztrades.com](https://coleztrades.com/) best in the space by far, come back a review here if you join.

replies.author: eagerchild
replies.body: Please p.m. me the other group you are using

replies.author: digots
replies.body: [https://coleztrades.com](https://coleztrades.com/)

replies.author: Free-Statistician911
replies.body: using stop loss is a killer because as much as something drops in 1 hour it can go up double in 2 hours, I lost loads using stop Loss it's not the one.

comments.author: Additional-Plant-259
comments.body: Hi I was going to join too but after reading this not sure . Is there anything out there that is reliable please, I joined Apollo Capital assets with 100k prop account and only traded 2 days since middle of July with 199.00 profit . Their support it very poor and no contact with other people. Should I give up any hope of making money from trading or can you guys advise of a good company/group I would really appreciate your advice thanks

replies.author: Pinotr36
replies.body: Try Alchemist, seems to do a steady % every week, albeit small

comments.author: Pinotr36
comments.body: Hi, has anyone any updates on D.O.T Trading?

replies.author: Direct_Impression864
replies.body: Following...

replies.author: billybob2917
replies.body: Following

comments.author: Bernard-Toast
comments.body:
I made over 6k profit in two months using both the Foundation and the Accelerator version.  Yes, there are bad days, but then there are really good days.  One morning I made £1,200 in an hour.  The trend is constantly upwards, with a few dips here and there.

I would personally highly recommend it.

replies.author: Signal-Resort-8164
replies.body: Hi what do you now think to dot trading ?

comments.author: DiN007UK
comments.body:
I have been a member of DOT for the past 4/5 months.

As people mentioned about the trading experience..
* Often double down and haven't not SL (Stop Loss) or TP (Take Profit)
* Mostly very few trades and definitely not a BOT or AI involved.
* They have had dips, but have mostly delivered weekly gains (some bad weeks included)
* Mostly they trade $GOLD (XAUUSD) & GER40 markets.
* They show no market knowledge within the Telegram posting (mostly all sales spam)

Absolutely Zero value joining the Telegram channel.
It is full of no value posts encouraging you to deposit more or the referral scheme they do.

It's nothing special about what they are doing.
All you need is a StarTrader account (Hedge STP is the account they make people open).
You can just Copy trade them.
(They are very reliant on broker sign up commission and try and make you change the Affiliate code, so they can re-earn commission)

They charge 10% profit share for the 'DOT Trading' copy. The 'DOT Accelerator' is a massive 35% profit share.
If they generate a loss, then they have to recover that before they earn Profit share again.

They have recently been investigated by the UK FCA and they have a warning on the FCA website.
Suspect this is why no more advertising on Instagram.

I still follow them, but have limited the money in the account.
Definitely not worth engaging with them on Telegram.
They seem like children and just full of sales spam and no market knowledge.

replies.author: ReturnAdventurous218
replies.body: How did you find out that they not using AI Bot? Also do you have a link that they have a warning on the FCA Website.

replies.author: DiN007UK
replies.body:
Have a look at the TrustedReviews website or just Google it 
The FCA link is available via Google 

From the trading style it very much seems like a human using signals rather than automation or Algorithm trade.

But they have seen mostly profitable on a weekly basis. With the occasional bad weeks

replies.author: DiN007UK
replies.body: https://www.fca.org.uk/news/warnings/dot-trading-dont-overthink-trading-dontoverthinktrading

replies.author: [deleted]
replies.body: [removed]

replies.author: ReturnAdventurous218
replies.body: Thnx for link, sorry for the late reply. So you been with DOT Trading bot for 4/5 months, how much money have you made since you started? Would you recommend them?

replies.author: Pinotr36
replies.body: Be good to know

replies.author: Bernard-Toast
replies.body:
I'm making about a grand and a half a month.  I would definitely recommend it. 

I'm using both the foundation and the accelerator (which is much riskier and have made losses recently), and there is a new one called Overdrive, which hasn't traded yet.

replies.author: Pinotr36
replies.body:
Been a bad week so far…How much £ have you with them to make £1.5k a month? 
The overdrive allegedly make 2 to 3% a week but the commission they stop is 35%, Dark Algo is similar but they only take 2.5% commission

replies.author: Which_Artichoke_9964
replies.body: I'm curious about this as well, I joined Foundation at the start of September with £500 with an 100% account bonus so trading with £1000. It was gradually growing but the up and down jumps can be pretty large, but overall it's been in profit for most of the time. It was up to £830 and then in two days its back to £550 so it feels like square one. It seems to overleverage after losing trades to recoup losses, and i'm worried about another big loss so I've paused copying for now ( of course it will make a bit profit on the next trade, typical haha)  may resume later. It would be good to connect with others, on all of these telegram channels the clients can't communicate with each other.

replies.author: New-Job-2518
replies.body:
Have you had any luck making withdrawals lately? 
Account blown this morning

replies.author: Calm-Associate-3274
replies.body:
Sorry but it's all a scam. I left a review on Trustpilot about it. When I joined, I only had £400. After almost a week I had lost £40. Nothing staggering but it still seemed like a lot for a week. Something in my gut told me to pull my money and I am glad I did. Here are the red flags for me:


-Losses are never (or barely mentioned) in the group. Even with the losses, the admin would still state they had a successful week overall. Huh?? How?


-I read the reviews on Trustpilot and it's where it gets interesting. There are a staggering amount of positive reviews but they are all written so generically. Almost like a ChatGPT post. Perfectly written. Posts like: "I never need to worry about trading anymore because DOT does the work for me in the background. Thank you, DOT." And if you read a lot of them, they all sound eerily similar. Paid reviews? Probably.


-This is where it gets interesting. There are bunches of negative reviews hitting recent weeks and they all talk negatively of how they lost a lot of money, trades too high, accounts blown. But the responses from DOT all say things like: "Not sure why you would post something like this as we are up for the week." Why do they never admit to any losses, which is part of trading anyway? Just say you had a bad month.. but no. Never admit it.


-I did some digging into the screenshots on telegram and tbh, you will be disappointed - but probably not shocked - to find these same screenshots actually belong to other traders. Likely real traders. To top it off, the images of people going on expensive holidays and yada yada yada...I also image reverse looked them up...they all came from Instagram stars or places like Pinterest. So the images aren't real and neither are the screenshots.


-After I left my negative review, the DOT admin/guys/whoever messaged me on telegram to say he was happy to help me out with anything and if I would be willing to give it another shot. (This was before TP was flooded with negative comments). I had a think and said. "Okay, maybe I didn't give it a proper chance." The next minute he replies. "Before we start over, can you remove your review or at least edit it to 3 stars?" When I said I wouldn't but would change it if things improved, his response was. "Yeah but now I have explained things, can you at least change to 3 stars?" Why would I do that? 3 stars indicates an average score. Something is satisfactory. That's it. And then he said: "It just makes us look bad." Boom! That was is. That was the confirmation I needed, above all else, that the whole thing is scammy and all they care about is building a positive reputation to reel in more gullible people.


I apologise that this post is a bit long but honestly, I would pull your money out now if you can and find something else

replies.author: SliceyBoiiii
replies.body: So do u think the people who responded to the post could be lying?

replies.author: Calm-Associate-3274
replies.body:
Honestly, I am just going off my own experience but I have never known a company to beg me to change my review because it "makes them look bad." Any reputable company would take all reviews - good and bad - on the chin. And should never ask a user to change their review especially if it's fair and based on their own experience.


So it makes me wonder if a lot of the reviews are paid/fake. And yeah, it's difficult to even know if the people in the comments are being honest or if they are also paid to toot DOT's horn

comments.author: Big-Association2472
comments.body:
D.O.T. Trading (Don't Overthink Trading) is considered a scam and is not a regulated entity. The Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) has issued a warning against it, noting that dealing with the firm means you will not be protected by the Financial Ombudsman Service or the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS). This lack of regulation means your money is at high risk if the firm goes out of business. 
Why D.O.T. Trading is considered a scam 
Unregulated: The FCA has warned that D.O.T. Trading is not authorized to provide financial services in the UK.
No investor protection: As a result, there are no legal protections in place to safeguard your funds. You would not have access to the Financial Ombudsman Service or the FSCS if something goes wrong.
Lack of transparency: The company has not displayed any valid regulatory information or a physical office address on its website, which are signs of an unreliable entity.

comments.author: WordAwkward7764
comments.body: Just look on StarTrader at the follower accounts and you can see there profitability. It’s just a follow account, better off signing up direct and copying those with a better record
